steps.title: Read the local context
steps.content: Simply modifies arranged and is paired with neatly, so it describes the plan's arrangement as straightforward and uncomplicated. The next sentence adds that Clara did not know how to begin, creating a contrast between a plan that seemed easy and an execution that was difficult.

steps.title: Check meanings against context
steps.content:
• Choice A would mean weakly or barely, which does not fit a plan called excellent and neatly arranged.
  • Choice B suggests rushed action, but the passage emphasizes simplicity, not speed.
  • Choice D would make the plan foolish, contradicting the positive description before the difficulty is noted.
The answer is C.
